commit.message:
feat: UX polish — breadcrumbs, TOC, collapsible sections, mobile tables

7 UX improvements based on user POV audit:

1. Jump-to TOC bar — auto-built from h2 headings, shows on pages with 3+ sections
2. Collapsible h3 skill sections — click to expand/collapse (▾ indicator)
3. Breadcrumbs — Home > Cert Study Guides > AZ-900 (SEO + orientation)
4. Mobile table scroll — tables now horizontally scrollable on small screens
5. 'Who is this exam for?' moved to top (before Quick Facts) on AZ-900
6. Removed duplicate Quick Links section from AZ-900 (Study Resources covers it)
7. Listing page subtitle: value-driven ('52 exams...') not instructional ('click on card')

All fixes apply to all 52 study guide pages via the shared template.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve error message when app identifier is empty (#362)

When `client-id` (or the deprecated `app-id`) resolves to an empty
string, for example because a secret or variable is not available in the
workflow context, the error message from `@octokit/auth-app` is not very
helpful:

```
[@octokit/auth-app] appId option is required
```

A validation check was added recently to catch this earlier, but its
message could be more informative:

```
Either 'client-id' or 'app-id' input must be set
```

This updates the message to clarify that the value resolved to empty and
nudges users toward checking their secret or variable availability:

```
The 'client-id' input must be set to a non-empty string. If using a secret or variable, ensure it is available in this workflow context.
```

Closes #249

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat/bootstrap makefile (#91)

* feat(deploy): add make bootstrap target with per-environment Flux Kustomizations

Replace single deploy/flux/kustomization.yaml with per-environment files
(kustomization-dev.yaml, kustomization-staging.yaml, kustomization-prod.yaml)
and add a `make bootstrap [environment]` target that applies FluxCD source
resources and environment-specific Kustomizations.

- `make bootstrap` applies GitRepository + ImageRepository source resources
- `make bootstrap production` applies sources + infra Kustomizations + env overlay
- Supports aliases: production→prod, development→dev
- Update docs, README, and test matrix to reference new file structure

* fix(deploy): update stale comment in flagger-kustomization.yaml

Reference kustomization-{env}.yaml instead of removed kustomization.yaml.

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Capabilities panel for MCP servers, tools & skills

New panel (🔌 Capabilities button in control bar) shows:
- MCP Servers with status dots (connected/disabled/pending/failed)
  and toggle switches to enable/disable per session
- Tool chips inside each MCP server card, showing which tools
  each server provides (click to exclude/include)
- Skills section with enable/disable toggles
- Filter input to search across all capabilities
- Collapsible sections with enabled/total count badges

Backend: Added bridge methods for MCP list/enable/disable, tools
list, and skill disable. Server handles list_capabilities,
toggle_mcp, and toggle_skill WebSocket messages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: embed Lua 5.3.6 compiler — no external luac dependency

- Bundle Lua 5.3.6 C source and build 32-bit/64-bit helper executables
  at compile time via cc crate (same approach as luatos-tools)
- Helpers are embedded via include_bytes!() and cached at runtime in
  user's local app data / XDG cache directory
- Replace find_luac() external search with embedded compiler invocation
- compile_lua_bytes() API: source bytes in, bytecode out via stdin/stdout
- Flash commands (bk7258, xt804) now read bitw/use-luac/use-debug from
  SOC info.json and compile .lua files automatically during flash
- Add SocInfo convenience methods: script_bitw(), script_use_luac(),
  script_strip_debug() with chip-based defaults (XT804→64, BK72xx→32)
- Add tests for 32/64-bit compilation, strip mode, syntax errors
- Verified on real Air8101 hardware (COM6): embedded luac compiles and
  flashes correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix %ThrowTypeError% intrinsic: dispatch, frozen, non-extensible

- Add 'Function.prototype.restrictedThrow' handler to call_host_fn
  so the thrower actually throws TypeError when called directly
- Freeze the %ThrowTypeError% object: non-writable/non-configurable
  name and length, __frozen__, __sealed__, __non_extensible__ flags
- Same thrower instance shared for getter and setter (per spec)

Cluster 38 (ThrowTypeError): 7 → 1 failure (6 tests fixed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add mihomo-proxy-setup skill

Add a new skill for installing and managing a Mihomo-based proxy in
user-space. Supports Linux (systemd) and macOS (launchd), with:

- Mihomo binary + yq auto-download for correct OS/arch
- Clash subscription integration with auto-update timer
- MetaCubeXD Web UI panel
- Proxy wrapper scripts (with-proxy, proxy-agent, proxy-copilot, proxy-claude)
- Interactive parameter collection (ports, secret, subscription URL)
- Full uninstall flow
- Troubleshooting guide

No root/sudo required.

commit.message:
ci: parameterize sideload build identifiers

The workflow was still baking in the repo bundle IDs and App Group, which only
work for the Apple account that owns those identifiers. That matched the repo,
but not the user-specific Xcode flow required for working App Group sharing.

This updates the GitHub Actions workflow to accept an app bundle ID and App
Group ID for workflow_dispatch builds, rewrite the project and plist metadata to
match those values, and then package an IPA with matching entitlements for the
app and extension. The README now documents the sideload workflow and why the
identifiers must belong to the final signing account.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix timeline: non-overlapping cards, lifespan bars, restore Turing

- Sort mathematicians by birth year; compute non-overlapping Y positions
  per side (left/right) so no two cards ever overlap
- Add Gantt-style lifespan bars in the center using greedy lane assignment,
  colored by field — shows who was alive at the same time at a glance
- Clicking a bar selects that mathematician
- Selected bar highlighted; others dim to 65% opacity
- Card offset from center now scales with the number of lanes
- Turing was hidden by Bézier (same birth year slot) — now both visible

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: ToggleSelected deletes schedule, RemoveAsync leaks config, dead DI

Bug 1 (severe): ScheduleForm.ToggleSelected called RemoveScheduleAsync to
disable a schedule. RemoveScheduleAsync previously called
_configService.DeleteSchedule — so toggling a schedule off permanently erased
it from configuration. Fix: ToggleSelected now always calls
AddOrUpdateScheduleAsync (which handles both enable and disable paths in
Quartz without touching config). Falls back to SaveSchedule if no scheduler.

Bug 2 (concern separation): SyncSchedulerService.RemoveScheduleAsync was
calling _configService.DeleteSchedule. Callers that want to delete from
config already do so explicitly (ScheduleForm.DeleteSelected). Removing the
call from RemoveScheduleAsync keeps each layer responsible for its own
persistence. DeleteSelected still calls _configService.DeleteSchedule
explicitly before removing the Quartz job.

Bug 3 (dead code): Program.cs registered ScheduleForm as a transient DI
service, but ScheduleForm is always constructed with 'new' in ShowSchedulesDialog
with explicit deps. The DI registration was never resolved and is removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: SyncAsync double-processes conflicts via ExecuteSyncAsync

SyncAsync() resolved conflicts and moved them to plan.Uploads/Downloads,
then called ExecuteSyncAsync(). ExecuteSyncAsync calls ResolveConflictsAsync,
which checks plan.HasConflicts (= plan.Conflicts.Count > 0). Because
plan.Conflicts was never cleared, ResolveConflictsAsync fired
ConflictsDetected again and appended duplicate Upload/Download operations.

This affected the SyncJob (scheduled sync) code path.
The MainForm two-step path (CalculateChangesAsync + ExecuteSyncAsync) was
unaffected because it never called SyncAsync.

Fix: clear plan.Conflicts after SyncAsync finishes processing them and
before calling ExecuteSyncAsync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: awss3-sync

commit.message:
fix: ConflictResolutionForm ApplyAll does not visually update grid

ApplyAll() modified row.Resolution on the backing List<ConflictRow> then
called _grid.Refresh(). Because List<T> does not implement
INotifyPropertyChanged, the DataGridViewComboBoxColumn cells retained their
old displayed values — the quick-apply buttons appeared broken.

Fix: reset DataSource to null then reassign the list, forcing the
DataGridView to re-read every cell value from the data source.
The underlying data was always correct; only the visual feedback was wrong.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Helper Substitution (#8357)

* Agent: Helper Substitution

* refactor: replace bespoke cloneAstNodeWithoutTraversalLinks wrapper and inline Set dedup with Core helpers

Before:
- `cloneAstNodeWithoutTraversalLinks<T>(node: T): T` in lint rule-base-helpers
  was a trivial pass-through that just called `Core.cloneAstNode(node) as T`,
  adding no behavior over the established Core helper.
- `[...new Set(filePaths)]` in refactor-engine reimplemented array deduplication
  inline when `Core.uniqueArray()` already provides this with small-array
  optimizations.
- optimize-math-expressions-rule used `CoreWorkspace.Core.*` while other lint
  rules used the shorter `Core.*` namespace pattern.

After:
- Callers in optimize-math-expressions-rule and optimize-logical-flow-rule
  use `Core.cloneAstNode()` directly with proper MutableGameMakerAstNode casts.
- refactor-engine uses `Core.uniqueArray(filePaths)` for both the
  globalvar-to-global and loop-length-hoisting codemods.
- optimize-math-expressions-rule imports `{ Core }` consistently.
- Tests updated to exercise `Core.cloneAstNode` directly.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SimulatorLife/GMLoop/sessions/5bed553d-7b97-40bd-8c37-6f56344d2748

commit.message:
security: add Strategy 4 to detect cross-directory attacks

When cwd is outside the project, bash commands with absolute paths
to project files were undetected. Strategy 4 extracts absolute paths
from bash commands and walks up from each to find .agents/ directory.

Now catches: rm /path/to/project/file from home directory.

51/51 tests passed (5 + 15 + 31 including 4 cross-directory tests).

Data layer overhaul: PokeAPI, caching, offline, dark mode, cameos

Major data layer changes:
- Add PokeAPI integration (src/lib/pokeApi.ts) for species data and
  evolution chains with proper name handling (Mr. Mime, Farfetch'd, etc.)
- Search cards by nationalPokedexNumbers instead of broken name parsing,
  fixing trainer-owned Pokemon (Marnie's Grimmsnarl = Grimmsnarl #861)
- Add IndexedDB cache layer (src/lib/apiCache.ts) with TTL support
- Add request scheduler (src/lib/requestScheduler.ts) with concurrency
  control, rate-limit handling, retry, and request deduplication
- Add card deduplication after multi-source fetches
- Fix card number sorting for promo/alphanumeric numbers

Service worker and offline:
- Add vite-plugin-pwa with workbox runtime caching for PokeAPI,
  TCG API, card images, and Google Fonts
- Previously viewed data works offline

Evolution chain UX:
- Remove evolution chain toggle from Pokemon selector
- Show evolution chain inline in search results
- Click Pokemon name to add just that one
- Click chain chip to add full evolution line

Dark mode:
- Add system-preference dark mode via prefers-color-scheme media query

Cameo database:
- Import 345 cameo appearances for 91 Pokemon from community spreadsheet
- Add import script (scripts/import-cameos.mjs) to regenerate from CSV
- Simplify CameoBrowser to work with new CameoRecord data format

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(graph-manager): use native async FalkorDB API

Replace sync FalkorDB client + threadpool workaround with the native
falkordb.asyncio API. Uses BlockingConnectionPool shared across requests,
asynccontextmanager for ephemeral_graph, and awaited query calls.

- graph_manager: sync → fully async with connection pool
- executor: remove run_in_executor, use async with ephemeral_graph
- main: add lifespan handler to close pool on shutdown

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(device): use media_device type instead of receiver

media_device is semantically more accurate for music players.
Same capabilities, better voice phrases ('включи плеер' vs 'включи ресивер').
